what_it_does: Defines a single reusable React composer input surface with three visual variants (hero, bar, inline), shared leading mark and action buttons, optional starting-point chips, and controlled/uncontrolled value handling.
observations: Unifies welcome prompt, studio Ask-bar, and interview composer into one component driven by a variant prop.
Embeds ComposerButton and ComposerMark as small reusable sub-components.
Supports both controlled (value/onChange) and uncontrolled (internal useState) modes.
Provides attach, image, mic, and send action buttons with a consistent Icon component.
Includes optional chips list below the input for hero-style starting points.
Adds a data-flip='composer' attribute for shared-element morph transitions.
Exports ComposerButton and Composer to the global window object.
Uses a useStateCo alias for React.useState.
standards: Single component replacing three near-duplicate bars.
Consistent leading Vi sparkle mark and identical action buttons across all variants.
data-flip attribute wired for morph/transition logic.
Controlled/uncontrolled input pattern in one surface.
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props.
Default prop values for variant, placeholder, and defaultValue.
Aria-label derived from title for action buttons.
concerns: Icon component is used but not defined in this file.
Window global exports may conflict with module bundlers or tests.
send() only clears internal state in uncontrolled mode; controlled callers must reset value themselves.
No validation or loading/disabled state for attach/image/mic actions.
setVal uses a ternary expression inside a block; works but is slightly unusual.
notes: This is the shared input surface meant to look and behave the same across welcome, studio, and interview contexts while supporting morph transitions.
questions: Where is the Icon component defined or imported?
What consumes the data-flip='composer' attribute for morph animations?
Are the composer--hero, composer--bar, and composer--inline styles defined in a separate stylesheet?
Is the window global export required by the surrounding app architecture?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of consolidating three near-duplicate composers into one variant-driven component matches the implementation.
intention_signals: Reduce duplication and drift between welcome, studio, and interview input bars by making one composer component whose mark, buttons, and behavior stay identical across screens and morph cleanly during navigation.
novelty: The variant-driven unification of three distinct composer contexts with a data-flip hook for shared-element morphing.
contribution: A reusable, variant-aware composer surface with embedded mark and button components, optional starting-point chips, controlled/uncontrolled value handling, and a data-flip marker for transition-aware rendering.
summary_for_embedding: A React composer component providing hero, bar, and inline variants with shared mark/action buttons, optional chips, controlled/uncontrolled input, and a data-flip attribute for morph transitions.
intention_for_embedding: To unify the welcome prompt, studio Ask-bar, and interview composer into a single consistent input surface that can morph between screens.

symbols.does: Renders a small icon action button for the composer.
symbols.description: A reusable button with an Icon, title/aria-label, optional disabled state, and a kind prop that switches between ghost and send styling; used for attach, image, mic, and send actions inside the composer.

symbols.does: Renders the leading Vi sparkle mark.
symbols.description: A static span containing a sparkle Icon with aria-hidden='true'; provides the consistent leading visual mark across all composer variants.

symbols.does: Updates the composer value in controlled or uncontrolled mode.
symbols.description: A setter closure that calls onChange when the component is controlled and otherwise updates the internal useState value, enabling both controlled and uncontrolled usage patterns.

symbols.does: Submits the trimmed composer value when it is non-empty.
symbols.description: Validates that the current value has non-whitespace content, then calls onSubmit with the trimmed string and clears the internal state only when running in uncontrolled mode.
